reasoning: The question asks if the 20-year TIPS yield (DFII20) will increase between 2026-03-29 and 2026-04-05. Both these dates are Sundays, meaning the effective comparison will likely be between the values on Friday 2026-03-27 and Friday 2026-04-03. The most recent value as of 2026-03-27 was 2.57%, which represents a sharp increase from 2.36% on March 19. While daily Treasury yields closely resemble a random walk with roughly a 50% chance of an increase over a short horizon, the recent sharp upward spike (21 bps in just over a week) suggests a higher likelihood of short-term mean reversion or consolidation rather than continued steady increase. The statistical trend analysis from FRED projects a slight decline to 2.538 by the target date, yielding a combined model estimate of 0.35 for an increase. Balancing the random walk (martingale) prior of 0.50 with the mean-reversion expectation after a sharp jump and the statistical projection, I estimate the probability of a further increase at 41%.
